mnemonics: King Philip Came Over For Good Spaghetti
Parents Just Don't Understand Kids These Days
keyPoints: Sequential searching is a linear search algorithm that checks each element in the list one by one.
It starts from the first element and compares it to the target value.
If the values match, the algorithm stops and returns the index of the match.
If the values do not match, the algorithm moves to the next element and repeats the process.
Sequential searching has a time complexity of O(n), where n is the number of elements in the list.
It is simple to implement but can be slow for large lists.
